title: My experience of re watching NGE 8 years later
display_title: My experience of re watching NGE 8 years later
wikitext:
[]: My experience of re-watching NGE 8 years later             Quote Post by Nitecon » Mon Oct 10, 2005 5:32 am Hello, I am new to the boards, this site is fantastic Eva-Monkey, kudos to you!! Okay, now the formalities are out of the way, here is what I want to discuss. I first watched NGE when it was first aired in Hong Kong in 1997-1998 (I think). Back then, I was only 17-18 years old and it was just another cool Japanese anime series, I didn't really pay attention to the whole storyline or understand most of the concepts discussed/put across. I must admit that back then I always felt like I was missing episodes or something as it didn't seem to have much continuity between episodes except the increment in the Angel number (as a teen, I was... well a teen, damn those hormones). When I watched the ending, it didn't make sense to me, but I left it at that and didn't look into any further. Fast forward to 2005. I have just purchased the DVD box-set with the two movies as well. Sitting through the whole series, then watching EoE in one day, without flinching was, an experience. It has come to a point where this series has had a massive impact on my life in a positive way. I could not believe what I missed 8 years ago, and I don't think anything I have ever watched has ever had me think/re-think over and over again, the ideaology/discussions/topics that are depicted in NGE. Thanks for reading, and I hope that I can learn more from everyone on here. By the way, on a unrelated topic to the above, did anyone else after watching the series want to take Misato under their wing and look after her? I just had the urge to comfort her and keep her safe (curse my weakness for vulnerable girls).

[]: Re: My experience of re-watching NGE 8 years later             Quote Post by geishaboy » Mon Oct 10, 2005 6:36 am Nitecon wrote: By the way, on a unrelated topic to the above, did anyone else after watching the series want to take Misato under their wing and look after her? I just had the urge to comfort her and keep her safe (curse my weakness for vulnerable girls).

[]:             Quote Post by Nitecon » Mon Oct 10, 2005 7:09 am What I found very amusing and scarily similar to myself was one specific trait of Shinji's that was touched upon early on in the series. The ability he had (or lack of for any other answer) to be content with saying "Yes" to all of Misato's questions when she berates him after he disobey's her order. His contentment to just float through life without conflict, acknowledging everything, and being indifferent was quite true to life. :cry: Being unable to express his feelings on anything, all bottled up until the s**t really hits the fan later on in the series........ poor chap.

[]: Re: My experience of re-watching NGE 8 years later             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Mon Oct 10, 2005 2:01 pm Nitecon wrote: I could not believe what I missed 8 years ago, and I don't think anything I have ever watched has ever had me think/re-think over and over again, the ideaology/discussions/topics that are depicted in NGE.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Mon Oct 10, 2005 2:42 pm Yes to your Misato question. SO you watched EVA in hing kong so you seak japoanese then? Good translators are alway welcome.

[]: Re: My experience of re-watching NGE 8 years later             Quote Post by Carl Horn » Mon Oct 10, 2005 7:05 pm Mr. Tines wrote: Hi there! I think NGE is a series that is more effective when you've had the chance to make the some of the same sort of screw-ups the characters have, and can watch them stepping obliviously into disaster; even without revisiting the eschatology..

[]:             Quote Post by Nitecon » Tue Oct 11, 2005 3:36 am DatDude wrote: SO you watched EVA in hing kong so you seak japoanese then? Good translators are alway welcome.

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Tue Oct 11, 2005 8:42 am Hooray! My first post >.> I am about to do the same thing that you did... Only difference is that it has only been... 3 years or so since I last saw it. The first time I saw it I think I was between 12 and 14 years old... Boy that screwed me up. It has come to a point where this series has had a massive impact on my life in a positive way.

[]:             Quote Post by insight » Tue Oct 11, 2005 11:48 am Hey Eternal Yamcha. Were you ever on deck-zone a while back. Because I was a poster on that forum and I tend to remember your name.

[]:             Quote Post by DatDude » Tue Oct 11, 2005 6:31 pm Nitecon wrote: DatDude wrote: SO you watched EVA in hing kong so you seak japoanese then? Good translators are alway welcome.

[]:             Quote Post by insight » Wed Oct 12, 2005 7:12 am You're going to have to pay me in order to be accepted. If you don't you are always going to have that cold feeling of rejection and it will make your life miserable. I accept checks, money orders, paypal, and Visa. *crosses arms*

[]: Re: My experience of re-watching NGE 8 years later             Quote Post by Digitalex » Wed Oct 12, 2005 4:53 pm Carl Horn wrote: That's why I made sure I was 25 before I saw it. â€”C.

[]: ^_^ \/,,             Quote Post by chiefen » Wed Oct 12, 2005 8:42 pm Nitecon wrote: DatDude wrote: SO you watched EVA in hing kong so you seak japoanese then? Good translators are alway welcome.

[]:             Quote Post by Oblivious » Thu Oct 13, 2005 7:16 pm I first saw the series when I was around 17 too. I am now about 18. I tend to refer to my life in BE(Before Eva) and AE(After Eva). All in all, Eva was a wakeup call for me, into a matrix world.

[]:             Quote Post by insight » Thu Oct 13, 2005 8:17 pm Oblivious wrote: I tend to refer to my life in BE(Before Eva) and AE(After Eva).

[]: Re: My experience of re-watching NGE 8 years later             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Thu Oct 20, 2005 7:38 pm Nitecon wrote: Hello, I am new to the boards, this site is fantastic Eva-Monkey, kudos to you!! Okay, now the formalities are out of the way, here is what I want to discuss. I first watched NGE when it was first aired in Hong Kong in 1997-1998 (I think). Back then, I was only 17-18 years old and it was just another cool Japanese anime series, I didn't really pay attention to the whole storyline or understand most of the concepts discussed/put across. I must admit that back then I always felt like I was missing episodes or something as it didn't seem to have much continuity between episodes except the increment in the Angel number (as a teen, I was... well a teen, damn those hormones). When I watched the ending, it didn't make sense to me, but I left it at that and didn't look into any further. Fast forward to 2005. I have just purchased the DVD box-set with the two movies as well. Sitting through the whole series, then watching EoE in one day, without flinching was, an experience. It has come to a point where this series has had a massive impact on my life in a positive way. I could not believe what I missed 8 years ago, and I don't think anything I have ever watched has ever had me think/re-think over and over again, the ideaology/discussions/topics that are depicted in NGE. Thanks for reading, and I hope that I can learn more from everyone on here. By the way, on a unrelated topic to the above, did anyone else after watching the series want to take Misato under their wing and look after her? I just had the urge to comfort her and keep her safe (curse my weakness for vulnerable girls).

[]:             Quote Post by poetic_assassin » Thu Nov 10, 2005 5:03 pm I've kinda had the same thing happen to me, what with the revisiting the show years later. Cartoon Network had "Giant robot week". There was Gigantor, Nedesico, and Evangelion. It showed the first two eps of Evangelion and that was it. I waited 5 years for that show to air and it's finally come! Bless ya.

[]:             Quote Post by defiate » Thu Nov 10, 2005 5:11 pm I'm from Hong Kong too, and what makes (to whoever said it) think that people from Hong Kong could natrually translate Japanese? (A common misconception among Americans is that Hong Kong is in Japan... WRONG) ...ironically I speak Japanese and work in a Japanese restaurant in Switzerland now :? Anyways I'm 18 now, and I've watched Eva ever since 1997... which makes me 10 when I saw it for the first time. Amazingly they decided to air the series on a children's channel on cable TV in Hong Kong which is similar to a blend of Nickoledeon, Disney Channel and Fox Kids in the US. Kinda makes you wonder if they ever screen things before airing it. (NOTHING was edited. I don't know how other children would have reacted to the mutilation of Eva03 and the 14th angel battle, not to mention the dozens of floating Rei being disemboweled) I was CONFUSED AS HELL as a child but nevertheless I continued to pursue Eva until now (thank god I love giant mechs). Recently I ordered the platinum DVD set and watched the whole thing and MAN does that bring back nostalgia. I was laughing through most of EoE because of things I said whilst watching it when I was 12... stuff like "OH MY GOD BOOBIES!!" hahah also I though Eva was like a prophecy or something so I actually spent time to prepare for the Second Impact before Sept. 13 2000... made a little pillow bunker in my room. In hindsight I doubt that would have saved me from a duck. Ahhh fond memories of childhood stupidity. I think my childhood/adolescence was partially scarred by watching Eva too young :( I blame my parents. :P Also, I was disappointed kid when I found out Death/Air and EoE will not air in Hong Kong cinemas... I still am. Has anyone else started watching Eva when they were really young?

[]:             Quote Post by superdoughboy4 » Thu Nov 10, 2005 9:59 pm defiate wrote: I'm from Hong Kong too, and what makes (to whoever said it) think that people from Hong Kong could natrually translate Japanese? (A common misconception among Americans is that Hong Kong is in Japan... WRONG) ...ironically I speak Japanese and work in a Japanese restaurant in Switzerland now :? Anyways I'm 18 now, and I've watched Eva ever since 1997... which makes me 10 when I saw it for the first time. Amazingly they decided to air the series on a children's channel on cable TV in Hong Kong which is similar to a blend of Nickoledeon, Disney Channel and Fox Kids in the US. Kinda makes you wonder if they ever screen things before airing it. (NOTHING was edited. I don't know how other children would have reacted to the mutilation of Eva03 and the 14th angel battle, not to mention the dozens of floating Rei being disemboweled) I was CONFUSED AS HELL as a child but nevertheless I continued to pursue Eva until now (thank god I love giant mechs). Recently I ordered the platinum DVD set and watched the whole thing and MAN does that bring back nostalgia. I was laughing through most of EoE because of things I said whilst watching it when I was 12... stuff like " OH MY GOD BOOBIES!! " hahah also I though Eva was like a prophecy or something so I actually spent time to prepare for the Second Impact before Sept. 13 2000... made a little pillow bunker in my room. In hindsight I doubt that would have saved me from a duck. Ahhh fond memories of childhood stupidity. I think my childhood/adolescence was partially scarred by watching Eva too young :( I blame my parents. :P Also, I was disappointed kid when I found out Death/Air and EoE will not air in Hong Kong cinemas... I still am. Has anyone else started watching Eva when they were really young?

[]:             Quote Post by Eternal Yamcha » Fri Nov 11, 2005 8:09 pm I don't remember how old I was when I started watching it. I remember my very first taste of Evangelion came when I was either 11 or 12. He found a picture of a red haired girl that was wearing a suit on a yellow background and he was all like "Dude, her name is Asuka Langley (forgetting to mention her real last name)" I don't remember how I reacted, I didn't think she was cute back then. Hell, while I entered puberty early (I had a mustache at what... 12 or 13), it wasn't until I was 12 that I found girls attractive, both real and animated (first anime girl I found hot was Misty from Pokemon >.>) It wasn't until I think I showed my friend the picture of Asuka that he mentioned that he knew which series she was from. So when I stayed the night at his house we rented the first VHS of the series and watched it like, twice. I don't know if that was when I was 12 or 13. But I'll admit, that was a bit too young for my personal tastes to have started on it. It took me a year to watch just about the rest of the series. I didn't see EoE until I was 15 (my friend downloaded it) and then didn't get it in English until I was 16. Didn't see D&R until I was that age too. I mean, I seriously believe I ended up a better person because of Evangelion (for a period of time, the only thing that kept me alive was the series), because I had a traumatic event happen to me when I was 11. So, I used what I knew about the series and started forming my philosophies on life and began to get over the shit that was running through my head. But I do think it did scar me, Evangelion serious effed up my life. But it's better that then the path I was going. Right now I'd either be a goody two-shoes (as some kid called me when I was 12) or dead (I was terribly depressed from 11 to I think, 16.) I'm pretty much happy with who I am, sure there are a few things I would like to change, but that's all within the material realm. I don't have a job (which is a bitch to get in Oregon due to our high unemployment rate) and have absolutely no money. But again, that's all materialistic. (A common misconception among Americans is that Hong Kong is in Japan... WRONG)
